summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering IT infrastructure upgrades, surplus vehicle disposals, and a road closure for the Light Up Goshen Parade. County Commissioner David Painter provided updates on federal funding legislation, the Nestle Purina construction project, and regional infrastructure developments. The board also went into executive session to discuss employment and compensation of public employees.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed
Motion to consolidate December meetings from two to one passed
Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter/EMT John Lahneman effective October 19, 2021 passed
Motion to declare surplus and sell 2007 Chevrolet Trailblazer (VIN last four: 3740) on govdeals.com passed
Motion to declare surplus and sell 2004 Chevrolet Tahoe (VIN last four: 5859) on govdeals.com passed
Motion to approve road closure on November 20, 2021 for Light Up Goshen Parade on Linton, State Route 28, and Goshen Road passed
Motion to approve IT services contract with InTrustIT at average monthly cost of $5,564 starting December 1, 2021 passed
Motion to reappropriate $25,280.25 from general fund CDBG line item to contract and services for IT repairs and maintenance passed
Motion to enter executive session under R.C. 121.22 G1 to discuss employment and compensation of public employees passed
Motion to adjourn at 8:03 p.m. passed
action_items: Pass information to Chad about donating a dozen chairs for the columbarium from the Hawes family
Steve to prepare the budget for next week's meeting
Contact House representatives to support Senate Bill S3011 for ARPA funding flexibility
Coordinate with InTrustIT for December 1 onboarding with one-month overlap with current IT contractor
Consider adding 'Goshen Township' identification on back of new ambulances
Fire department to coordinate November 20 house burn training with East Fork, Loveland, and Milford departments
key_amounts: $5,564 average monthly cost for new IT services with InTrustIT
$11,000 annual increase over current IT services cost
$25,280.25 reappropriation from CDBG line item to contract and services for IT repairs
$550 million Nestle Purina construction cost ($754 million total project price)
1,200,000 square feet Nestle Purina facility under construction
197 acres purchased by Nestle plus 200 additional adjacent acres
notable_topics: New IT services contract with InTrustIT replacing current provider
IT infrastructure repairs and reappropriation of funds
Surplus vehicle disposal (2007 Trailblazer and 2004 Tahoe)
Light Up Goshen Parade road closure November 20, 2021
Resignation of part-time firefighter/EMT John Lahneman
County Commissioner Painter update on Senate Bill S3011 (ARPA flexibility)
Nestle Purina construction project update in Clermont County
Clermont County Trustees Association meeting and jail/training facility tour
Columbarium use and donation of additional chairs
Body camera upgrades for police department at no additional cost
December meeting consolidation from two meetings to one
Executive session on employment and compensation
Fire department house burn training and Holly Fair November 20
Village dissolution discussions (Newtonsville completed, Owensville on ballot)
attendees: Cheryl (filling in for Laura as fiscal officer)
notes: Trustee Allen was absent due to a sick family member. The transcript has multiple roll call errors with names not matching known trustees (e.g., Musil, Croninger, Rowe, Acasio, Coonerty, Leibovitz) suggesting transcription inaccuracies. Bob Hawes expressed gratitude for township staff support during what appeared to be a family loss/emergency involving someone named Jake. The fire department's new shed is nearly completed. Police body cameras are being upgraded to the newest model at no additional cost per the 2019 contract. There was humorous discussion about the color of the township ambulances (lime yellow vs. green). Reference to Owensville village dissolution being on next week's ballot.
title: BOT Mtg 10.26.21.mp4
